text: ate ni kan nye ti biemmoa, ase tama ale kan jam namsi ni alege nye ni a magsi ale basi ate ni cheŋ ale suiyɔgni la. Ate yɔgyɔgla de Naawen a niak fu la.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Wen a nye ba kama a basi wenzukku ayen tɛŋka zuk a nya,
translated: When he knows that he has done his work, he sees the strong one come to take it.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Mi a yaali ayen Wen a te fu wenzuk maglim ale tɛŋzuk jigsika kpari ŋanta ale da paalik.
translated: The person went to Wen's house to fetch the medicine and the tools, but they were not there.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Javuk meena ale bo tɛŋka zuk yaa kpi. Nuinsa ale gɔi duŋsa ale yeri duŋsa ale ŋan yirinta ale nurubiik meena a jam kpi kama.
translated: Javuk River flows through the valley and the fields. Nuinsa is near the village where the people and their families live alongside the river that runs through the valley and the fields.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: The translation of the Buli text is: "Dila Wen comes to take nuru with wadek, wadek and they do diila and you, nidoa they nipok and you do." Note: This is a speculative translation as Buli language expertise is limited.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa, come quickly to the river with Wen.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: Abraham said, "I know that Sarah my wife bore Isaac, and now she has given birth to him. I will follow the path of the Lord, for I have chosen to walk in His ways and keep His covenant as my eternal promise."

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob came to the place where there was an almond tree and a date palm tree, and the young girl was there eating, so he went to her. He also went to the daataŋa, and they were there with peaches, which they ate.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob goes to his house in the morning, and Wen's mother goes to her own house.

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: M le sum niak fu abe m basi ate fi ŋaaŋsaŋa a piisi ase chiŋmarisaŋa ale bo wenŋmazukku ale tintain busuŋ ale bo beli po la. Fi ŋaaŋsaŋa ale tuesi ba dachaasaŋa tɛŋsaŋa a tara.
translated: The matter has been taken up so that I may know the truth, through the ŋaaŋsaŋa and piisi, the chiŋmarisaŋa, the wenŋmazukku, the tintain busuŋ, and the beli po. The ŋaaŋsaŋa has been handled according to the proper procedures and methods.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ate nuruwa yaa weeni wa ayen. <<Basi mu ate m cheŋ, ale ka vari ale a veenti mu>> Alege Jekob a tulisi wa ayen, <<Fi dan kan niak mu, m kan basi fu ate fi a cheŋŋi.>>
translated: The lamp of the body is the eye. "If your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light." The prophet Jacob said this, "If you have light in you, you will have light in your heart, but if your eye is bad, you will have darkness in your heart."

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Joksan jam ka Siiba ale Dedan ko. Dedan ŋaaŋbisa jam le Assur demma ale Letus demma ale Leummi demma.
translated: Joksan came to Siiba and Dedan. Dedan then went to Assur, Letus, and Leummi.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Abimelek yaa weeni ayen, <<Mi tɛŋka ale la a doa fi niŋ, kali fi le a yaali kauk kuui la.>>
translated: Abimelek said to them, "I know that this is my task, but they have eaten the fruit."

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ate Aizik yaa jam basi Jekob suik, ate wa cheŋ Paddan Araam, Laban ale ka Betuel biik la ate wa chim Araam denɔ la, Iisɔ ale Jekob mawa Aribeka toawa la jigi.
translated: And Isaac sent Jacob away, and he went to Paddan-Aram, to Laban, the son of Betuel, from the city of Aram. Isaac had sent Jacob to take a wife from the house of Aribeka.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: Dila Wen comes to you with light and power, the path that unites Dila and you, and the way that leads you.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Now you know that in the river, the people come here.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: And the word of the Lord came to Abraham, saying, 'As for Sarah your wife, I will indeed give you a son by her; you shall call his name Isaac.' So Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said to himself, 'Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? And shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?'

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jakob came and took a handful of almonds and dapeelita, and he brought them to yɔgsisa, and they ate them there. He also came to daataŋa, and they went there and the people were waiting for him to arrive.
